case_title: JULIET GRIER VS. SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC,

docket_entries.description: CIVIL CASE COVERSHEET FILED (TRANSACTION ID # 100175349) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRIER, JULIET AN INDIVIDUAL
REPLY MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100187099) FILED BY DEFENDANT SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC
PROOF OF SERVICE FOR NOTICE OF APPEARANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 100187517) FILED BY DEFENDANT SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") IS: DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 30, 37, 48, 3, 14, 21, 22, AND 34. AS EXPLAINED IN THE DEMURRER, PLAINTIFF PLEADS A COGNIZABLE CLAIM TO RECOVER THE BONUS AND FUNDS FROM THE PROFIT-SHARING PLAN. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS (FAC 40, 43 (SECOND PAR. 43), 50, AND PRAYER (D). A PARTY CANNOT RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES FOR A CONTRACTUAL BREACH. (SEE CIVIL CODE SEC. 3294(A).) A PARTY ALSO MAY NOT RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES FOR A LABOR CODE VIOLATION. (SEE BREWER V. PREMIER GOLF PROPERTIES (2008) 168 CAL.APP.4TH 1243, 1252 ["PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE WHEN LIABILITY IS PREMISED SOLELY ON THE EMPLOYER'S VIOLATION OF THE LABOR CODE STATUTES"]; CLEVELAND V. GROCERYWORKS.COM, LLC, 200 F. SUPP.3D 924, 964 (N.D. CAL. 2016) ["CALIFORNIA COURTS HAVE HELD THAT PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE UNAVAILABLE FOR LABOR CODE VIOLATIONS."].)DENIED AS TO THE SPECIAL DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS. THE FAC IS PLEADED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY AND DEFENDANT RAISES A MATTER OF PROOF. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2023 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2023 Set For Trial On Dec-04-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2023 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Paraskevi Batsikas Esq. 415-262-0096 THE OTTINGER FIRM, P.C.for Plaintiff.
Dixie Trauber, Esq. 916-447-9200 Orick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms Trauber argues bonus payment. Ms. Batsikas responds. Both argue.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling on both motions as follows:

Defendant SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC's motion to strike the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is:

Denied as to paragraphs 30, 37, 48, 3, 14, 21, 22, and 34. As explained in the demurrer, plaintiff pleads a cognizable claim to recover the bonus and funds from the profit-sharing plan.

Granted without leave to amend as to the punitive damages allegations (FAC 40, 43 (second par. 43), 50, and Prayer (D). A party cannot recover punitive damages for a contractual breach. (See Civil Code sec. 3294(a).) A party also may not recover punitive damages for a Labor Code violation. (See Brewer v. Premier Golf Properties (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1243, 1252 ["punitive damages are not recoverable when liability is premised solely on the employer's violation of the Labor Code statutes"];
Cleveland v. Groceryworks.com, LLC, 200 F. Supp.3d 924, 964 (N.D. Cal. 2016) ["California courts have held that punitive damages are unavailable for Labor Code violations."].)

Denied as to the special damages allegations. The FAC is pleaded with sufficient particularity and defendant raises a matter of proof.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

MINI MINUTES:

Dixie Trauber, Esq. 916-447-9200 Orick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms Trauber argues bonus payment. Ms. Batsikas responds. Both argue.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling on both motions as follows:

DEFENDANT SPARK CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
